codeReview.summary: The fix commit correctly resolves the original blocking issues from both prior failed features: createContact no longer uses an unsafe cast, all server createContact callsites now provide a userId fallback, and interaction methods now require userId and enforce user-scoped WHERE clauses. However, the new seed-user fallback lookup is not actually constrained to the seed user and can resolve an arbitrary user row.

issues.description: getSeedUserId() uses `SELECT id FROM users LIMIT 1` instead of querying the seed user identity. In multi-user environments this can assign unauthenticated contact creates/imports/webhook contacts to a non-seed account, violating the feature requirement to use the seed user ID fallback.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance requires seed-user fallback for unauthenticated contact creation paths, but does not state how to resolve the seed user deterministically (e.g., by known seed username).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md migration guardrail says to use seed user fallback; scripts/seed.ts defines an explicit seed identity (`SEED_USERNAME = "brian_placeholder"`), while server/routes.ts resolves fallback with `from(users).limit(1)`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library notes document that contacts.user_id must be assigned, but omit the concrete seed identity lookup detail needed to implement safe fallback behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/relationshipos-schema-notes.md describes mandatory userId assignment but does not capture the seed-user identifier/pattern from scripts/seed.ts.

summary: Fail. The fix addresses all previously reported blockers around createContact enforcement and interaction user scoping, but introduces a new blocking correctness issue: fallback user resolution is non-deterministic (`LIMIT 1`) and does not guarantee the seed user ID.
